title: Alpha Decay Chart — Per-Trade Forward Return Distribution Tracker
summary: Practitioner research (markrbest.github.io, citing Timothy Masters) describes alpha decay charts as a technique to measure the distribution of returns over the lifetime of a trade, sampled at regular intervals. The key insight is that a trade with no drawdown going directly to profit is far superior to one that recovers after a deep intraday dip — and the alpha chart captures this. For Decifer, plotting forward return distribution at T+1, T+3, T+5, T+10 bars after entry for each signal type reveals the actual half-life of the signal and the optimal exit point before mean-reversion kicks in. Research from MicroAlphas (2025) confirmed swing-trade signal effectiveness diminishes after 3–10 days.
